setting.note: One hotel, three decades. A count sentenced in 1922 to house arrest lives out the whole Soviet era inside the Metropol, just off Theatre Square. The map is a single block: the hotel and its rooms, with the Bolshoi, Theatre Square, and the Kremlin walls at Red Square a few steps beyond the revolving doors.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Amor Towles, first published 2016. This book is mapped as a cited work: we store none of its text and reproduce no passages. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: A Gentleman in Moscow, Amor Towles (2016). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author or publisher.

palette.note: The deep claret of the Boyarsky and a bottle of Chateauneuf-du-Pape kept back from the commissars. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
